---
title: "BADM_Final_Project"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
date: "2025-04-27"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Load Required Libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(corrplot)
library(xgboost)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)

data <- read_csv("D:/BI@DM/Final/Global_Cybersecurity_Threats_2015-2024.csv")

data_clean <- data %>%
  select(Year, `Financial Loss (in Million $)`, `Number of Affected Users`, 
         `Incident Resolution Time (in Hours)`) %>%
  drop_na() 
print(paste("Number of columns:", ncol(data)))
print(paste("Number of rows:", nrow(data)))

```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r data}
summary(data_clean$`Financial Loss (in Million $)`)

summary_by_year <- data_clean %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_loss = mean(`Financial Loss (in Million $)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_loss = median(`Financial Loss (in Million $)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_loss = max(`Financial Loss (in Million $)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_loss = min(`Financial Loss (in Million $)`, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
print(summary_by_year)
```
```{r}
colnames(data)
```
```{r}
ggplot(summary_by_year, aes(x = Year, y = mean_loss)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "red", size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Trends in Financial Losses from Cyberattacks (2015-2024)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Mean Financial Loss (in Million $)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(data_clean, aes(x = factor(Year), y = `Financial Loss (in Million $)`)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "orange", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Yearly Distribution of Financial Losses",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Financial Loss (in Million $)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
numeric_data <- data_clean %>% select_if(is.numeric)
corr_matrix <- cor(numeric_data, use = "complete.obs")
corrplot(corr_matrix, method = "circle", type = "upper", tl.cex = 0.8)
```
```{r}
features <- as.matrix(data_clean[, -c(2)]) # Exclude Financial Loss as it's the target
labels <- data_clean$`Financial Loss (in Million $)`
train_matrix <- xgb.DMatrix(data = features, label = labels)
xgb_model <- xgboost(
  data = train_matrix,
  max_depth = 6,
  eta = 0.3,
  nrounds = 100,
  objective = "reg:squarederror"
)
importance <- xgb.importance(feature_names = colnames(features), model = xgb_model)
xgb.plot.importance(importance_matrix = importance, top_n = 5, measure = "Gain")
```
```{r}
ggsave("trends_financial_losses.png")    # Line plot of trends
ggsave("yearly_distribution_boxplot.png") # Boxplot of yearly distributions
ggsave("feature_importance_plot.png")   # XGBoost feature importance plot

write.csv(summary_by_year, "summary_by_year.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
```{r}
ggplot(data_clean, aes(x = Year, y = `Financial Loss (in Million $)`)) +
  geom_line(stat = "summary", fun = "mean", color = "blue", size = 1) +
  geom_point(stat = "summary", fun = "mean", color = "red", size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Trends in Financial Losses from Cyberattacks (2015-2024)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Mean Financial Loss (in Million $)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
attack_percentage <- data %>%
  count(`Attack Type`) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = n / sum(n) * 100)

print(attack_percentage)
```
```{r}
ggplot(attack_percentage, aes(x = "", y = Percentage, fill = `Attack Type`)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar("y", start = 0) +
  labs(
    title = "Percentage of Attack Types"
  ) +
  theme_void()
```
```{r}
affected_users_analysis <- data %>%
  group_by(`Attack Type`) %>%
  summarize(
    Total_Affected_Users = sum(`Number of Affected Users`, na.rm = TRUE),
    Percentage = (Total_Affected_Users / sum(data_clean$`Number of Affected Users`, na.rm = TRUE)) * 100
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Percentage)) 
print(affected_users_analysis)
```
```{r}
ggplot(affected_users_analysis, aes(x = "", y = Percentage, fill = `Attack Type`)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar("y", start = 0) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(Percentage, 1), "%")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    color = "white",
    size = 4
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Percentage of Affected Users by Attack Type"
  ) +
  theme_void()
```
```{r}
defense_analysis <- data %>%
  group_by(`Attack Type`, `Defense Mechanism Used`) %>%
  summarize(Count = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(Count))

print(defense_analysis)
```
```{r}
ggplot(defense_analysis, aes(x = `Attack Type`, y = `Defense Mechanism Used`, fill = Count)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of Attack Types and Defense Mechanisms",
    x = "Attack Type",
    y = "Defense Mechanism",
    fill = "Frequency"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
```{r}
ggplot(defense_analysis, aes(x = `Attack Type`, y = Count, fill = `Defense Mechanism Used`)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  labs(
    title = "Defense Mechanisms Used Against Attack Types",
    x = "Attack Type",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
```{r}
write.csv(defense_analysis, "defense_mechanism_analysis.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
```{r}
source_analysis <- data %>%
  group_by(`Attack Source`) %>%
  summarize(
    Total_Loss = sum(`Financial Loss (in Million $)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    Average_Loss = mean(`Financial Loss (in Million $)`, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Loss))
print(source_analysis)
```
```{r}
attack_loss_analysis <- data %>%
  group_by(`Attack Type`) %>%
  summarize(
    Average_Loss = mean(`Financial Loss (in Million $)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    Total_Loss = sum(`Financial Loss (in Million $)`, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Loss))
print(attack_loss_analysis)
```
```{r}
geographic_analysis <- data %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  summarize(
    Total_Loss = sum(`Financial Loss (in Million $)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    Number_Attacks = n()
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Loss))
print(geographic_analysis)
```
```{r}
ggplot(data, aes(x = `Incident Resolution Time (in Hours)`, y = `Financial Loss (in Million $)`)) +
  geom_point(color = "blue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Resolution Time vs. Financial Loss", x = "Resolution Time (in Hours)", y = "Financial Loss (in Million $)") +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}

industry_analysis <- data %>%
  group_by(`Target Industry`) %>%
  summarize(
    Average_Loss = mean(`Financial Loss (in Million $)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_Loss = max(`Financial Loss (in Million $)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min_Loss = min(`Financial Loss (in Million $)`, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Average_Loss))
print(industry_analysis)
```

```{r}


```
```{r}


```
```{r}


```
```{r}


```
